stories.title: QA-ticket JSON schema + check.mjs validation
stories.summary: Landed `schemas/qa-ticket.schema.json` (every field from S092 design §4) and extended `scripts/backlog/check.mjs` to walk `backlog/qa-tickets/*.qa-ticket.json` (skipping `archive/`), parse + validate via the existing AJV instance, and emit AGF_QA_TICKET_PARSE / _SCHEMA / _DUPLICATE_ID diagnostics. Empty inbox is silent. Conditional rule baked into the schema: when type='regression-needed', regressionFor must be set.
verification: acceptance: Drop a hand-written `backlog/qa-tickets/QA-2026-05-20-001.qa-ticket.json` with the required fields; `npm run backlog:check` validates it cleanly. Break it (drop `repro`, set `type: 'regression-needed'` without `regressionFor`) — check.mjs reports AGF_QA_TICKET_SCHEMA with the right path.
schemas/qa-ticket.schema.json (17 properties, additionalProperties:false, allOf conditional)
scripts/backlog/check.mjs adds a QA-ticket walk; reuses the sprint AJV instance
+5 unit tests under tests/unit/qa-ticket-schema.test.ts covering valid pair, missing regressionFor, missing repro, bad id pattern, unknown severity
Live integration smoke verified: dropping a valid ticket into backlog/qa-tickets/ → 0 errors; dropping the invalid fixture → AGF_QA_TICKET_SCHEMA surfaces with the right path

stories.title: scripts/backlog/qa-ticket.mjs new "<title>" scaffold helper
stories.summary: scripts/backlog/qa-ticket.mjs implements the `new` subcommand: writes a fresh `QA-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN.qa-ticket.json` (date in UTC; NNN auto-incremented past the highest existing for today) into `--into <dir>` (default `backlog/qa-tickets/`). Pre-fills agfFormatVersion=1, filedAt=now, id, title (from positional arg). Flags: `--severity`, `--type`, `--found-in-pr`, `--found-in-sprint`, `--regression-for`. Body uses literal `TODO: ...` placeholders for repro / expected / actual / summary so a `grep TODO backlog/qa-tickets/` lists every unfinished ticket, AND the placeholders are long enough to satisfy schema minLength so the file passes backlog:check on first save. Refuses to clobber an existing path. Prints the absolute path to stdout for `$EDITOR` piping. New npm script `qa:ticket` wraps the CLI.
verification: acceptance: `node scripts/backlog/qa-ticket.mjs new "Bot suicides on fire-up pickup" --severity major --type bug --found-in-pr 100` writes a new file under backlog/qa-tickets/, prints its path, validates clean via backlog:check.
+6 unit tests under tests/unit/qa-ticket-cli.test.ts: id pattern, NNN auto-increment, schema-valid output, regression-for flow, no-clobber path, short-title rejection
npm run qa:ticket -- new ... script wired in package.json

stories.title: scripts/backlog/promote-qa.mjs — tickets → pending-sprint stories
stories.summary: scripts/backlog/promote-qa.mjs walks `backlog/qa-tickets/*.qa-ticket.json`, validates each against schemas/qa-ticket.schema.json (aborts on any failure — no partial writes), filters by `--min-severity`, and builds one story per ticket. Story ids follow `BUG-<UPPERCASE-SLUG>-<NNN>` / `REGRESSION-<UPPERCASE-SLUG>-<NNN>` to satisfy the sprint schema's `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9-]*$` pattern. NNN auto-increments past existing story-id collisions. The story `summary` embeds a fenced QA-repro block (severity, filedAt, repro steps, expected, actual, logs, screenshot, playtest, source ticket id) so the sprint render carries the full reproducer. Bug↔regression-test pairs are linked via `dependsOn` so the bug fix can't be claimed until the regression test exists. After the write, the script runs `scripts/backlog/check.mjs --json` inline; on any error it restores the original sprint JSON and exits non-zero. Successful promotion archives source files via `renameSync` into `backlog/qa-tickets/archive/<sprint-id>/`. Flags: `--into S0NN` (required, must be pending), `--min-severity`, `--dry-run`, `--qa-dir <path>` / `--sprints-dir <path>` / `--skip-check` (test-time overrides). New npm script `qa:promote`.
verification: acceptance: Drop two fixture qa-tickets (one bug, one regression-needed linked via regressionFor); run `node scripts/backlog/promote-qa.mjs --into S<next-pending>`; the pending sprint JSON gains two stories with the bug's dependsOn pointing at the regression test, source files move to archive/, backlog:check stays green.
Live dogfood verified against S091: 2 tickets promoted, archived, then reverted via git checkout for cleanup
+7 unit tests in tests/unit/qa-promote.test.ts cover happy path, dependsOn linking, --dry-run, --min-severity filter, archive move, pending-only target enforcement, empty inbox
npm run qa:promote wraps the CLI; production callers do NOT pass --skip-check (test-only flag)

stories.title: backlog:check warns when an implemented story lacks `acceptance:` line
stories.summary: scripts/backlog/check.mjs gains a soft rule: every story.status='implemented' must have a first verification[] entry whose trimmed text matches `/^acceptance\s*:/i`. Missing acceptance fires AGF_BACKLOG_NO_ACCEPTANCE warning. Restricted to non-archived sprints — backfilling 80+ archived sprints would drown the output (~600 warnings, confirmed). New work (active + pending sprints) is enforced from now; the legacy clean-up is a follow-up sprint when we want it.
verification: acceptance: A fresh sprint JSON with an `implemented` story whose verification[] lacks an `acceptance:` line surfaces AGF_BACKLOG_NO_ACCEPTANCE under `npm run backlog:check`. Adding `acceptance: <text>` as the first entry silences it.
Case-insensitive match — 'Acceptance:', 'ACCEPTANCE:' both silence the warning
Archived sprints skipped (legacy stories don't drown the report)
+3 unit tests under tests/unit/qa-acceptance-rule.test.ts: archived sprints silent; pending sprint warns on missing prefix; case-insensitive silence path

stories.title: engine doctor surfaces QA inbox count + critical-age warning
stories.summary: Extend `engine/tools/doctor/project-doctor.ts` with a `qaInbox` block populated by walking `backlog/qa-tickets/*.qa-ticket.json` from repo root. Report: total tickets, breakdown by severity, ids of the oldest critical and oldest major. Recommendation when any `critical` ticket has filedAt older than 24h (suggests dev to promote ASAP). The format pass prints the section under `QA inbox:` after the existing Backlog block.
verification: acceptance: With two fixture qa-tickets in the repo, `npm run engine:doctor -- examples/kaboom-crew` prints a `QA inbox: 2 tickets (1 critical, 1 major)` block. With zero tickets, the block is omitted entirely.
Recommendation surfaces for critical tickets older than 24 hours
+1 unit test on a fixture inbox

stories.title: QA agent onboarding docs (agent.md + ticket-template.md + regression-promotion.md)
stories.summary: Write three concise markdown docs under `docs/qa/`: (1) `agent.md` — onboarding for a fresh QA Claude (what to read, what tools, file-ownership table from design §7, ticket schema link, the 'acceptance:' contract); (2) `ticket-template.md` — fill-in-the-blanks scaffold mirroring the schema; (3) `regression-promotion.md` — the heuristic flow chart from design §6 expanded with two worked examples (new-PR-only bug → no regression ticket; old-functionality bug → both tickets). Each doc < 250 lines.
verification: acceptance: A fresh agent loading docs/qa/agent.md can identify, in under a minute, what files it owns, what schemas to follow, and how to file its first ticket.
All three docs exist under docs/qa/ and link to schemas/qa-ticket.schema.json + the design doc
Internal links resolved (no broken relative paths)

stories.title: Update CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md with the QA-workflow section
stories.summary: Add a §QA-Workflow block to `CLAUDE.md`: file-ownership table (dev writes engine/examples/tests/sprints/, QA writes backlog/qa-tickets/ + notes/qa/ + examples/**/playtests/qa-proposed/), the qa-intake/YYYY-WW branch convention, the rebase-from-main rule at story start. Update the §Working-Mode bullets accordingly. Add a one-line pointer in `AGENTS.md` so non-Claude agents notice the role exists. Keep both files as terse as possible.
verification: acceptance: CLAUDE.md has a discoverable §QA-Workflow section; existing bullets aren't reflowed/broken; the file-ownership table renders correctly.
AGENTS.md gains a one-line pointer to docs/qa/agent.md

stories.title: .claude/agents/qa-reviewer.md subagent definition
stories.summary: Land `.claude/agents/qa-reviewer.md`: name + description (so the parent agent can route to it via subagent_type), model (Sonnet — cheaper for verification work), tools allowlist (Read, Bash limited to read-only commands + qa-ticket.mjs + playtest runner, WebFetch off, Write restricted to backlog/qa-tickets/** + notes/qa/** + examples/**/playtests/qa-proposed/**), short system prompt that points at docs/qa/agent.md as the canonical onboarding read.
verification: acceptance: From a fresh repo state, `claude --subagent qa-reviewer` (or programmatic invocation via the Agent tool) loads the definition, refuses to write to engine/, accepts a `backlog/qa-tickets/new` task.
.claude/agents/qa-reviewer.md follows the same frontmatter shape as the existing playtest-runner.md / engine-architect.md
